config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tindakan yang merugikan orang kecil. Saya lebih setuju jika prioritasnya adalah menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama dengan perlindungan hak individu, bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for suppressing ordinary people. From my perspective in China, I understand why collective stability and public order are valued, especially in a large society where coordination matters, but I also think individual rights need real protection or they become too easy to sacrifice in the name of the greater good. The hard part is not choosing society or the individual as an absolute priority, but setting clear limits so public interests do not override basic fairness and dignity. If the statement means society can always win, I disagree with it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diutamakan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil, padahal justru hak individu itu yang mencegah kekuasaan mayoritas jadi sewenang-wenang. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi menurut saya hanya sah didahulukan kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak melanggar hak dasar seperti kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil. Jadi, yang paling masuk akal bukan menomorsatukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan batas yang jelas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over the rights of the individual in a blanket way. From my perspective in China, collective order and social harmony are important, and I understand why many people value them, but I have also seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to silence ordinary people or excuse unfair treatment. If individual rights are not protected with clear limits, then the so-called interests of society can become whatever those in power decide they are. For me, the right balance is not society first, but society and individual rights both constrained by fairness, dignity, and accountability.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu jika dimaknai secara mutlak, karena di Indonesia label “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil. Memang ada situasi tertentu—misalnya bencana, kesehatan publik, atau ketertiban umum—di mana kepentingan bersama bisa didahulukan, tetapi harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak merampas hak dasar. Kalau hak individu tidak dilindungi, mayoritas atau pihak berkuasa bisa semena-mena atas nama kebaikan bersama. Jadi menurut saya, yang benar bukan masyarakat selalu di atas individu, melainkan keduanya harus seimbang dengan batas yang tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society above the individual as a general rule, because in my view that is too easy to abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the emphasis on collective order, public stability, and social responsibility, and those values do matter in a large society, but they cannot be allowed to erase basic rights. The repeated point from the other side about "public good" being used against ordinary people is exactly why I am cautious: once rights are treated as secondary by default, power can define society’s interests however it wants. For me, society’s interests should matter, but only within clear limits of fairness, dignity, and real protection for the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
